102: **England**, top of **Group L** on seven points with a GD of +4, face **Argentina**, first in **Group J** with a perfect nine points and a GD of +7, in the second semifinal at Atlanta Stadium (12:00 PM PT) — the rivals' first competitive meeting in 24 years and, remarkably, the first time Lionel Messi has faced England in his senior international career despite over 200 appearances for the Albiceleste. **Argentina** required extra time to beat Switzerland 3-1 in the quarter-final — Julian Álvarez's 112th-minute long-range strike and a late Lautaro Martínez counter breaking the deadlock after Breel Embolo's red card — with Messi contributing an assist for Alexis Mac Allister's opener; two of their five knockout-stage games have now gone to extra time. **England** beat Norway 2-1 in the quarter-final after Bellingham's brace completed a comeback — his second consecutive knockout brace, a feat last achieved at a single tournament by Diego Maradona in 1986 — though Declan Rice was withdrawn at half-time through illness and a hamstring issue, Bukayo Saka is limited by fitness restrictions, and suspended Jarell Quansah and injured Jordan Henderson (broken arm) are both absent. The winner faces either **France** or **Spain** in the final at MetLife Stadium on July 19.
101: **France**, top of **Group I** with nine points from three wins and a GD of +8, face **Spain**, first in **Group H** on seven points with a GD of +5, in the first semifinal at AT&T Stadium in Arlington (12:00 PM PT). **France** beat Morocco 2-0 in the quarter-final — Mbappé scoring his eighth goal of the tournament before being withdrawn at 77 minutes with an ankle knock he has since called minor, with Michael Olise providing five assists across the campaign, more than any other player at this tournament — while Deschamps sweats on the fitness of Tchouaméni (muscle issue, missed two games) and Koné (knee, came off vs Morocco), though both are expected to be available. **Spain** needed an 88th-minute Mikel Merino substitute winner to beat Belgium 2-1 — Merino becoming the first player in tournament history to score the winning goal as a substitute in two different knockout matches — while Nico Williams and Yeremy Pino return to the bench from injury but are not expected to start and Víctor Muñoz remains a doubt; the sides have met only once at the tournament, France winning 3-1 in the 2006 round of 16, with Spain winning seven of the 11 encounters since. The victors face the **England** vs **Argentina** winner in the final at MetLife Stadium on July 19.
